summary: Descriptive overview of intoxication in substantive law: voluntary vs involuntary, specific intent crimes.
key_points: voluntary vs involuntary is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for intoxication are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine intoxication in litigation.
The doctrine of intoxication has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding intoxication requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
